content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as a recursive, self-improving agent in their own right, where the memory structure itself actively selects which new agent experiences to encode rather than passively storing all inputs. This turns the conventional hierarchy of 'agent uses memory' on its head, making memory a curious active learning agent that optimizes its own stored content for the parent agent's long-term task performance.
